items.title: Welcome Llama 4 Maverick & Scout on Hugging Face
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet:
Hugging Face's blog in early 2026 highlights several major model releases. The most prominent is Meta's Llama 4, which introduces a new auto-regressive Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with two variants: Llama 4 Maverick (17B active parameters out of ~400B total with 128 experts) and Llama 4 Scout (17B active parameters out of ~109B total with 16 experts). Overworld also released Waypoint-1, a real-time interactive video diffusion model controllable via text, mouse, and keyboard.

items.title: Introducing Meta Llama 3: The most capable openly available LLM to date
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source AI release is the Llama 4 model family, announced in early 2026. Llama 4 represents the beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI, featuring models like Llama 4 Maverick and Llama 4 Scout. These are Meta's first open-weight models built using a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, and the first natively multimodal models in the Llama series. They are available to download on llama.com and Hugging Face, with broad availability across cloud platf

items.title: AI Act | Shaping Europe's digital future
items.author: digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu
items.snippet: The AI regulatory landscape in 2026 is shaped by several major developments. In the EU, the AI Act is reaching full applicability on August 2, 2026, with rules for high-risk AI systems phasing in through August 2027. Transparency obligations are now in force, requiring providers of generative AI to ensure AI-generated content — particularly deepfakes and public-interest text — is clearly labelled and identifiable. This represents the most comprehensive binding AI law globally and is setting the

items.snippet: In early 2026, the AI agent framework landscape has seen significant shifts. Microsoft has merged AutoGen with Semantic Kernel into a unified SDK called the Microsoft Agent Framework, with 1.0 GA targeted for Q1 2026 — AutoGen itself is now in maintenance mode (bug fixes only, no new features). LangGraph released v1.1.2 on March 12, 2026, and remains the preferred choice for production multi-step pipelines requiring predictable, debuggable state machine control flow. CrewAI reached v1.10.1 on Ma

items.title: One Year Since the DeepSeek Moment
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet:
Hugging Face's blog in early 2026 highlights several major model releases. The most prominent is Meta's Llama 4, which introduces a new auto-regressive Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with two variants: Llama 4 Maverick (17B active parameters out of ~400B total with 128 experts) and Llama 4 Scout (17B active parameters out of ~109B total with 16 experts). Overworld also released Waypoint-1, a real-time interactive video diffusion model controllable via text, mouse, and keyboard.

The broad

items.title: GitHub - salttechno/LLM-Model-Comparison-2026
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: As of March 2026, the open source LLM landscape is highly active with several major releases dominating benchmark leaderboards. GLM-5 (Reasoning) from Z AI leads recent rankings with a Quality Index of 49.64, excelling in coding and reasoning tasks, with a 203K context window under an open license. Alibaba's Qwen3 series remains competitive at both the 30B A3B and 235B MoE tiers, while DeepSeek V3.2 and DeepSeek R1 Distill Llama 70B continue to rank among the top performers for large and mid-siz

items.title: 2026 AI Laws Update: Key Regulations and Practical Guidance | Gunderson Dettmer
items.author: www.gunder.com
items.snippet: The AI regulatory landscape in 2026 is shaped by several major developments. In the EU, the AI Act is reaching full applicability on August 2, 2026, with rules for high-risk AI systems phasing in through August 2027. Transparency obligations are now in force, requiring providers of generative AI to ensure AI-generated content — particularly deepfakes and public-interest text — is clearly labelled and identifiable. This represents the most comprehensive binding AI law globally and is setting the

items.title: An AI companion for everyone - The Official Microsoft Blog
items.author: blogs.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft has been actively advancing its AI Copilot ecosystem in early 2026. On March 9, 2026, Microsoft announced Wave 3 of Microsoft 365 Copilot, introducing expanded model diversity including Claude and next-gen OpenAI models, general availability of Agent 365 at $15/user/month, and the new Microsoft 365 E7 'Frontier Suite' at $99/user launching May 1. Work IQ — an intelligence layer that learns how users work, with whom, and on what content — powers next-generation agentic experiences acros

items.title: Artificial Intelligence Mar 2026 — arxiv.org
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week on arxiv.org, research in AI agents and LLMs continues at a rapid pace heading into late March 2026. A major highlight is the comprehensive survey paper 'Large Language Model Agent: A Survey on Methodology, Applications and Challenges' (arXiv:2503.21460), which provides a methodology-centered taxonomy linking architectural foundations, multi-agent collaboration mechanisms, and evolutionary pathways for LLM agents. Alongside this, the cs.AI and cs.MA listing pages for March 2026 show a

items.title: Trending Papers - Hugging Face
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: March 2026 has seen a surge in open-source AI model activity across Hugging Face and GitHub. Meta's Llama 4 family — including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick — launched as natively multimodal models built on Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture. Scout stands out with an industry-leading 10-million-token context window. Alibaba's Qwen3 line continues to push scale, with Qwen2.5-Max exceeding 1 trillion parameters via MoE and achieving 92.3% accuracy on AIME25 benchmarks, supporting 119 langu
